author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
Migration paused 2026-06-23. Surfaced during a Haiku-fan-out
attempt: `jj git init --colocate` + `jjf init` on a repo with
live source on `main` moves git HEAD to `refs/jj/root`,
wiping the working tree from git's point of view. Recovery
took manual `git symbolic-ref` + `git reset --hard` and lost
the operator's uncommitted edits in the asterinas-workspace
tree.

The bug is the SAME class as the `JJF_ALLOW_SELF_HOST` guard
on jjforge's own repo (`08cf14b`). The current storage choice
("files on a bookmark") forces a working-copy snapshot per
write, which in colocate moves git HEAD off `main`. The guard
is a workaround; for the asterinas tree, the workarounds are
(A) a permanent sibling clone, or (B) a real storage redesign
to out-of-tree refs (the git-bug pattern).

User picked B (research first, then re-attempt). Filed
`487536a storage-out-of-tree-refs` as a research ticket
under this epic + epic:mvp-storage. Adding `blocks` edge
from `487536a` to this ticket — migration won't ship until
the storage shape is decided.

The audit doc, the bug-fix work (four QA bugs closed
2026-06-23, tests 387 → 421), and the asterinas-side header
shape (961 .md files, ~96 epic dirs, header shape per
audit's mapping table) all carry over to the re-attempt.
No data lost from the planning side.

author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
2026-06-23: Research ticket `487536a` closed. Verdict is to
move storage off the `issues` bookmark to per-issue git refs
(`refs/jjf/issues/<id>`), which kills HEAD drift at the source.
Design doc at `docs/storage-out-of-tree.md`. Implementation
epic filed as `bd98097`.

This migration stays blocked on `bd98097` (v3 storage). The
research doc recommends waiting for v3 over shipping the
sibling-clone interim — the workaround would persist on every
read/write of asterinas data forever until v3, and the
asterinas markdown tree was already an archive long before the
migration attempt. ~3–4 sessions of v3 work; then re-attempt
the Haiku fan-out per `docs/host-asterinas-audit.md`.
